skill.name: aws-solution-architect
skill.description: Design AWS startup architectures with serverless patterns, infrastructure-as-code templates, cost estimates, and security guidance.
skill.summary: Design serverless AWS architectures, IaC plans, and cost controls for startup teams.
skill.author: alirezarezvani

security_audit.summary: Static findings were mostly false positives caused by AWS terminology, fenced shell examples, public documentation links, and IaC template strings. No malicious intent, credential exfiltration, subprocess execution, network client code, or prompt injection was found. The skill still needs a publication warning because generated IaC uses unsanitized names and broad API defaults that require review before deployment.

medium_findings.title: Unsanitized Names Interpolated into Generated IaC
medium_findings.description: The generator normalizes app_name only by lowercasing and replacing spaces, then inserts it into CloudFormation, CDK, and Terraform templates. A crafted name could corrupt generated templates or introduce unintended resources if callers use untrusted input.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The interpolation is clear and repeated across generated templates. Impact depends on whether callers pass untrusted app names and deploy the output without review.

medium_findings.title: Generated API Templates Use Broad Defaults
medium_findings.description: The generated CloudFormation template defaults CORS to all origins, and the CDK template allows all origins and methods. This is not malicious, but production users should restrict origins and methods before deployment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The broad CORS settings are directly visible in generated deployment templates. They are common starter defaults, so this is a deployment hardening issue rather than malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Static High-Risk AWS Keyword Matches Were False Positives
low_findings.description: C2 keyword, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance alerts matched AWS service names, architecture terminology, cost analysis fields, and sample outputs. No evidence found of command-and-control behavior, weak cryptographic implementation, or host reconnaissance code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review showed architecture recommendations and cost calculations, not malicious runtime behavior. The Python files do not import subprocess, networking libraries, or credential APIs.

low_findings.title: Documentation Command Examples Require User Judgment
low_findings.description: The external command findings are fenced examples for installing AWS tooling and deploying templates. They are legitimate instructions, but users should review generated infrastructure before running deployment commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible documentation examples for aws, cdk, npm, and terraform. There is no hidden shell execution by the skill itself.

low_findings.title: Public Resource Links and Credential Mentions Were Benign
low_findings.description: Network findings are links to AWS documentation, calculators, and sample repositories. Credential-related matches occur in security advice telling users to avoid hardcoded credentials.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The links are public AWS and GitHub resources, and no code sends data to them. The credential text is defensive guidance, not credential access.

low_findings.title: Script Pattern Is a Generated CDK Snippet
low_findings.description: The dynamic function constructor alert points to TypeScript CDK text embedded in a Python string. It generates a Lambda resource definition and does not execute dynamic code inside the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed context shows a generated CDK lambda.Function resource declaration. It is not Python eval, exec, Function constructor execution, or shell execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: Generated IaC Should Be Reviewed Before Deployment
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill can produce deployable CloudFormation, CDK, and Terraform text. Generated infrastructure can create real AWS resources, costs, and permissions if applied without review.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The generator methods directly return deployable IaC formats. This is intended functionality, but it creates operational risk when deployed blindly.

content.value_statement: Startup teams need secure AWS designs without overspending. This skill recommends serverless architectures, IaC options, cost controls, and deployment guidance.
actual_capabilities: Recommends AWS architecture patterns based on scale, budget, team experience, and application type.
Outlines serverless, three-tier, event-driven, data pipeline, IoT, and multi-region designs.
Generates CloudFormation, AWS CDK, and Terraform template text for common serverless stacks.
Estimates monthly cost ranges and identifies common optimization opportunities.
Provides security, monitoring, compliance, deployment, and migration checklists.
limitations: Cost estimates are approximate and must be validated with current AWS pricing.
Generated IaC needs human review before production deployment.
It does not inspect live AWS accounts or verify deployed resources.
Service availability and compliance requirements vary by region and workload.

use_cases.title: Plan an MVP Backend
use_cases.description: Design a low-cost serverless backend with API Gateway, Lambda, DynamoDB, Cognito, and basic monitoring.

use_cases.title: Prepare Production Infrastructure
use_cases.description: Compare architecture patterns and generate an IaC starting point for a scalable web or SaaS application.

use_cases.title: Reduce AWS Spend
use_cases.description: Analyze a resource inventory and prioritize cost savings across compute, storage, databases, and networking.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner Architecture Request
prompt_templates.scenario: You have a simple product idea and need a first AWS design.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a serverless AWS architecture for a web app with 5,000 monthly users, email login, file uploads, and a 300 USD monthly budget.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Architecture Options
prompt_templates.scenario: You need tradeoffs before choosing a stack.
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare serverless, three-tier, and container-based AWS architectures for a B2B SaaS product with 50,000 users and SOC 2 goals.

prompt_templates.title: Create an IaC Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: You are ready to turn a design into deployable infrastructure.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a CloudFormation implementation plan for an authenticated API using Lambda, API Gateway, DynamoDB, Cognito, CloudWatch, and least-privilege IAM.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Cost and Reliability Review
prompt_templates.scenario: You have an existing AWS environment and need optimization guidance.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this AWS inventory for cost, reliability, and security risks. Prioritize changes by savings, risk reduction, effort, and production impact.

output_examples.input: Design a mobile backend for 100,000 users with social login and real-time updates.
output: Recommended pattern: serverless mobile backend with Cognito, AppSync, DynamoDB, Lambda, and CloudWatch.
The response explains service choices, scaling limits, estimated monthly costs, and deployment phases.
It includes security controls such as least-privilege IAM, encryption, and audit logging.

output_examples.input: Find ways to reduce a 2,000 USD monthly AWS bill.
output: The skill groups recommendations by compute, storage, database, networking, and monitoring.
It highlights idle resources, retention policies, Savings Plans, and budget alerts.
Each recommendation includes estimated savings, priority, and implementation effort.

output_examples.input: Generate deployment guidance for a three-tier production web app.
output: The skill describes an ALB, ECS Fargate, Aurora, ElastiCache, CloudFront, S3, and Cognito design.
It provides phased rollout guidance, monitoring checks, rollback planning, and security review points.

best_practices: Provide scale, budget, compliance, traffic, and team experience before asking for a design.
Validate every generated template with AWS tools and peer review before deployment.
Ask for cost, security, reliability, and operational tradeoffs for each recommended service.
anti_patterns: Do not deploy generated IaC directly to production without review and testing.
Do not request a multi-region design before confirming real availability and latency needs.
Do not share secrets, account credentials, or private customer data in prompts.

faq.question: Can this skill deploy resources to AWS?
faq.answer: No. It generates architecture guidance and template text. Users must deploy resources with their own reviewed tooling.

faq.question: Does it support Terraform and CloudFormation?
faq.answer: Yes. The included generator can produce CloudFormation, AWS CDK, and Terraform style outputs for serverless stacks.

faq.question: Are the cost estimates exact?
faq.answer: No. They are planning estimates and should be checked with AWS Pricing Calculator and current account usage.

faq.question: Is it suitable for regulated workloads?
faq.answer: It can suggest controls for GDPR, HIPAA, SOC 2, and PCI-DSS, but legal and compliance teams must validate requirements.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide application type, expected users, request rate, data size, budget, compliance needs, regions, and team experience.

faq.question: Can it optimize an existing AWS bill?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide a resource inventory, monthly spend, utilization metrics, and current monitoring details for better recommendations.
